mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in >55% of months where FEDFUNDS declines, or rolling Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer spending, which reduces corporate earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in >60% of instances following the trigger, or the signal shows no statistical significance in out-of-sample backtests.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates broad-based domestic demand growth, which boosts corporate revenues and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to produce positive 60-day returns in >50% of triggered quarters, or the interaction term loses predictive power when added to a baseline model.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production reflects strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which signals near-term economic momentum and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in >55% of months where INDPRO increases, or the signal reverses direction during high inflation regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation forces markets to price in imminent monetary tightening, which compresses equity valuations and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in >55% of months following the inflation threshold breach, or the signal shows zero predictive accuracy in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls indicates expanding economic activity and rising household income, which supports corporate profit growth and yields positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative in >50% of quarters where PAYEMS increases, or the rolling Rank IC remains below 0.01 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in real GDP and real personal consumption expenditures confirms synchronized demand expansion, which reduces recession risk and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The combined signal produces negative 20-day returns in >55% of observed periods, or the interaction effect vanishes when controlling for monetary policy shifts.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained contraction in industrial production signals weakening manufacturing demand and potential economic slowdown, which triggers risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls below its 6-month moving average
failure_condition: Positive 5-day returns occur in >60% of instances following the trigger, or the signal fails to predict returns during periods of stable monetary policy.
